phase_name: Service Layer — Business Logic, Singleton & Decorator

subtask_summary.notes: AdminUserService uses Page.map() for idiomatic DTO conversion. @Transactional(readOnly=true) applied at class level. EntityNotFoundException from jakarta.persistence used consistently.

AdminPharmacyService implements AdminPharmacyServiceI. updateStatus fetches, sets, saves, publishes AdminActionEvent, then null-checks email before calling NotificationFactory. @Qualifier not needed here since this class is concrete.
LoggingAdminPharmacyService is @Primary and injects AdminPharmacyService (concrete) via @Qualifier('adminPharmacyService') to prevent circular dependency. Logs entry and exit for all three methods.

AdminDashboardService uses LocalDate.now().atStartOfDay() for prescriptionsToday boundary. treatmentRatePercent guarded against division by zero (defaults to 0.0). Javadoc documents the Singleton pattern.

GlobalExceptionHandler handles EntityNotFoundException(404), MethodArgumentNotValidException(400 with all field errors joined), AccessDeniedException(403), generic Exception(500 with ERROR log). Unified error envelope matches README section 5.3.

findings.description: LoggingAdminPharmacyService is @Primary and implements AdminPharmacyServiceI. If the delegate was injected as AdminPharmacyServiceI, Spring would inject the @Primary bean (itself) causing a circular dependency at startup.
findings.resolution: Delegate is injected as the concrete AdminPharmacyService class with @Qualifier. Spring resolves the concrete bean directly, bypassing the @Primary resolution, eliminating the circular dependency.
findings.lesson_learned: When building a Decorator as a @Primary bean, always inject the concrete implementation class (not the interface) to prevent circular injection. Use @Qualifier to be explicit.

findings.description: AdminPharmacyService.updateStatus() calls pharmacyRepository.save() inside a @Transactional method. If the async AuditLogListener fails (e.g., DB constraint), the main transaction is already committed and the audit log is silently lost.
findings.resolution: The async failure is caught and logged at ERROR level in AuditLogListener (from P2 finding fix). This is an accepted trade-off between availability and consistency for audit logging.
findings.lesson_learned: Async audit logging introduces eventual consistency. If audit completeness is critical, consider synchronous logging or an outbox pattern.

findings.description: AdminDashboardService.treatmentRatePercent uses prescriptionsToday/totalPrescriptions which measures daily volume as a percentage of all-time total. This is the formula specified in the README but may not reflect a meaningful 'treatment rate'.
findings.resolution: Implemented exactly as specified. No deviation from spec.
findings.lesson_learned: When implementing analytics metrics, validate the business formula with stakeholders before coding. The formula here comes from the course spec and was followed precisely.

recommendations_for_next_phase: P4-APIAgent: JwtAuthenticationFilter must exist in a shared security package before AdminSecurityConfig can reference it — create JwtUtil + JwtAuthenticationFilter in com.pharmacy.shared.security.
P4-APIAgent: AdminSecurityConfig must disable CSRF (stateless REST API) and set session policy to STATELESS explicitly.

P4-APIAgent: @AuthenticationPrincipal in AdminPharmacyController depends on the UserDetails implementation returning the User entity ID — verify the principal object exposes getId() or cast to the custom UserDetails class.
P4-APIAgent: @PreAuthorize requires @EnableMethodSecurity on the security config class — do not forget this annotation.
